pi_items.source: setup_task.sh Python data generation script explicitly appends sub-999 as an invalid responder.
Task description and verifier.py explicit threshold logic.
Explicitly checked in verifier.py copy_from_env calls.
setup_task.sh generates synthetic trial-level data directly using random.normalvariate() based on standard distributions.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on dynamically generated synthetic trial data, explicitly appending sub-999 as an invalid responder with randomized responses. However, due to the deterministic random.seed(42) in setup_task.sh, sub-999 randomly guesses target 45 times out of 70 AX trials, resulting in an uncorrected AX Hit Rate of ~0.643. This violates the prompts explicitly defined < 0.60 exclusion threshold. Consequently, a perfectly compliant agent should NOT exclude sub-999. If the agent follows the instructions correctly, it will fail the verifiers explicit 20-point check for sub-999 exclusion, but will correctly calculate the group means (which the verifiers internal ground-truth calculator also mistakenly computes across all 11 participants because its own threshold logic fails to exclude sub-999). The agent will thus pass the task with exactly 80/100 points.
